channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18449] Handling kernel mmap read-ahead triggering by advise(MADV_HUGEPAGE)
item.description:
kernel:LustreError: 126979:0:(rw.c:2162:ll_readpage()) ASSERTION( !ra_assert ) failed:

Found a possible bug in kernel to trigger readahead in Linux kernel:

static struct file *do_sync_mmap_readahead(struct vm_fault *vmf)
{
	struct file *file = vmf->vma->vm_file;
	struct file_ra_state *ra = &file->f_ra;
	struct address_space *mapping = file->f_mapping;
	DEFINE_READAHEAD(ractl, file, ra, mapping, vmf->pgoff);
	struct file *fpin = NULL;
	unsigned long vm_flags = vmf->vma->vm_flags;
	unsigned int mmap_miss;

#ifdef CONFIG_TRANSPARENT_HUGEPAGE
	/* Use the readahead code, even if readahead is disabled */
	if (vm_flags & VM_HUGEPAGE) {
		fpin = maybe_unlock_mmap_for_io(vmf, fpin);
		ractl._index &= ~((unsigned long)HPAGE_PMD_NR - 1);
		ra->size = HPAGE_PMD_NR;
		/*
		 * Fetch two PMD folios, so we get the chance to actually
		 * readahead, unless we've been told not to.
		 */
		if (!(vm_flags & VM_RAND_READ))
			ra->size *= 2;
		ra->async_size = HPAGE_PMD_NR;
		page_cache_ra_order(&ractl, ra, HPAGE_PMD_ORDER);
		return fpin;
	}
#endif

	/* If we don't want any read-ahead, don't bother */
	if (vm_flags & VM_RAND_READ)
		return fpin;
	if (!ra->ra_pages)
		return fpin;
        ...
}

Usually the kernel readahead will disabled by setting ra_pages with 0.
However,
when CONFIG_TRANSPARENT_HUGEPAGE is enabled and vm flagged with VM_HUGEPAGE, it will trigger the kernel readahead.
When the kernel is configed with CONFIG_TRANSPARENT_HUGEPAGE and the application is set VM_HUGEPAGE via MADV_HUGEPAGE via madvise() system call, it is possible to trigger these kind of read-ahead from Linux kernel and out the control of Lustre.
item.summary: Handling kernel mmap read-ahead triggering by advise(MADV_HUGEPAGE)

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: The follow-on patch was duplicating changes already merged so everything is complete.
Still a follow-on patch being tracked under this ticket

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57038/
Subject: LU-18449 llite: handle kernel readahead for VM_HUGEPAGE vma
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 4a9951dd7ea895930f5d5e264435e03f7b0f1930

"Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/62159
Subject: LU-18449 llite: fix wrong VM_HUGEPAGE readahead check
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 55d072001cad7a0700869d872af1e814f95041ee
I tried this patch on my environment, and it can fix the issue. Thanks! Qian

> Has the application used MADV_HUGEPAGE via madvise()?

I am not sure, the application suppose to use mmap to fetch the fetch the data.  

def load_safetensors_file(tensor_name, worker_id):
  # input dimension from vllm/model_executor/layers/linear.py:60 is 1
  #### way #1 where we load tensor, then narrow it
  loaded_weight = f.get_tensor(tensor_name).          <----- from here, I assume this is from tensorflow, which I am not familar with.
  if len(loaded_weight.shape) > 1:
    shard_size = loaded_weight.shape[1] // args.worker_count
    start_idx = worker_id * shard_size
    loaded_weight_narrowed = loaded_weight.narrow(1, start_idx, shard_size)
    param = torch.empty_like(loaded_weight_narrowed, device='cpu')
    param_data = param.data
    param_data.copy_(loaded_weight_narrowed)

Though I can reproduce the issue, do you have the patch based on 2.14?
Has the application used MADV_HUGEPAGE via madvise()?

I saw this in my testing.

[202727.315627] LustreError: 376530:0:(rw.c:2148:ll_readpage()) sbi ra pages 0, file ra pages 4096
[202727.315636] LustreError: 376530:0:(rw.c:2156:ll_readpage()) bdi io_pages 0
[202727.315640] LustreError: 376530:0:(rw.c:2162:ll_readpage()) ASSERTION( !ra_assert ) failed: 
[202727.315642] LustreError: 376530:0:(rw.c:2162:ll_readpage()) LBUG
[202727.315652] CPU: 18 PID: 376530 Comm: python3 Kdump: loaded Tainted: G           OE     -------  ---  5.14.0-427.31.1_lustre.el9.x86_64 #1
[202727.315655] Hardware name: Google Google Compute Engine/Google Compute Engine, BIOS Google 07/12/2025
[202727.315659] Call Trace:
[202727.315662]  <TASK>
[202727.315665]  dump_stack_lvl+0x34/0x48
[202727.315673]  lbug_with_loc.cold+0x5/0x43 [libcfs]
[202727.315717]  ll_readpage+0xc62/0xc90 [lustre]
[202727.315807]  ? __pfx_ll_read_folio+0x10/0x10 [lustre]
[202727.315871]  filemap_read_folio+0x40/0x2c0
[202727.315876]  ? xas_load+0x9/0xa0
[202727.315879]  ? __filemap_get_folio+0x106/0x330
[202727.315882]  filemap_fault+0x656/0x700
[202727.315886]  ll_filemap_fault+0x2e/0x50 [lustre]
[202727.315957]  vvp_io_kernel_fault+0x1b/0x310 [lustre]
[202727.316026]  vvp_io_fault_start+0x393/0xcd0 [lustre]
[202727.316093]  ? cl_lock_enqueue+0x56/0x110 [obdclass]
[202727.316213]  ? cl_lock_request+0x66/0x1d0 [obdclass]

I tested on rock9.4 with fio as follows:

 $TRUNCATE $file $((10 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024)) ||
                error "failed to truncate $file"
fio --name=read_test --ioengine=mmap --filename=$file --rw=read \
                --bs=1m --thp=1 || error "failed to run fio on $file"
cat /proc/meminfo | grep HugePage

It can not reproduce the panic...

The option "--thp=1" will inform the fio to run with MADV_HUGEPAGE madvise, I am just curious how the panic happened without the option "--thp=1"....

"Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57038
Subject: LU-18449 llite: handle kernel readahead for VM_HUGEPAGE vma
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: e56566635285c787c54363606c76452e37b31494
